response: We propose a novel method for [TASK], achieving state-of-the-art performance on the standard benchmark. Evaluated over five random seeds, our approach attains an average accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, outperforming a strong baseline (72.2 ± 0.9%) by +1.2 absolute points. While a two-sample t-test indicates that this improvement is not statistically significant (t = 1.04, p = 0.31; 95% CI: [–1.1, +3.5]), our method consistently matches or exceeds baseline performance across all trials. These results highlight the robustness and practical competitiveness of our approach, providing a promising new direction for [TASK]. Our analysis further identifies scenarios where the proposed method delivers the most pronounced gains, offering insights for future research. Overall, this work establishes a solid foundation for continued progress in [TASK], and demonstrates the value of [KEY NOVELTY] in advancing real-world performance.
